skill.name: docs-review
skill.description: Review documentation changes for compliance with the Metabase writing style guide. Use when reviewing pull requests, files, or diffs containing documentation markdown files.
skill.summary: Review Metabase documentation changes for style, clarity, structure, and reviewer-ready feedback.
skill.author: metabase

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but those hits are false positives from Markdown formatting, style-guide text, and tool names. The only real risk factor is that the skill declares Bash as an allowed tool, although the instructions do not request shell execution. No evidence of malicious intent, data exfiltration, prompt injection, or unsafe command construction was found.

low_findings.title: Bash Tool Permission Without Command Workflow
low_findings.description: The skill metadata allows Bash, which can execute external commands. The reviewed instructions do not ask the agent to run shell commands, build commands from user input, or execute downloaded code, so this is a low operational concern rather than a confirmed vulnerability.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The Bash permission is directly present in the skill metadata. I found no command workflow or injection path in the instruction body, which limits the concern.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The Ruby or shell backtick alerts are Markdown inline code, code fences, GitHub MCP tool names, and review comment examples. They are not executable code and do not form a command injection path.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain Markdown syntax and literal tool names, not Ruby code or shell execution. No user-controlled command construction is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Style Text Flagged as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts map to normal documentation text such as markdown, review formatting, descriptive links, and example wording. No cryptographic API, hashing function, key handling, or encrypted data flow appears in the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line context shows prose and examples only. There is no cryptographic operation to evaluate as weak or unsafe.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Writing Guidance Flagged as Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance alerts point to style guidance and PR review formatting instructions. The skill does not request network scanning, host discovery, environment enumeration, or system inventory collection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are documentation style rules and review output guidance. I found no semantic request for reconnaissance or discovery behavior.

content.value_statement: Documentation reviews often miss style, tone, and structure issues that affect reader comprehension. This skill applies the Metabase writing style guide and returns focused, numbered feedback.
actual_capabilities: Reviews documentation diffs, pull requests, or Markdown files against Metabase writing guidance.
Checks tone, clarity, structure, headings, link text, formatting, and instruction order.
Numbers every issue sequentially so authors can reference feedback easily.
Supports local review output when GitHub review tools are not available.
Uses pending GitHub review workflows when the required MCP tools are available.
Filters out feedback that would not materially improve the reader experience.
limitations: The skill is tailored to the Metabase writing style guide, not a generic documentation standard.
It does not verify product behavior beyond issues visible in the provided documentation changes.
GitHub review posting depends on compatible MCP tools being available in the session.
It can miss context that is outside the supplied files, diff, or pull request.

use_cases.title: Review pull request documentation
use_cases.description: Check changed Markdown files before merge and post numbered comments for actionable style fixes.

use_cases.title: Prepare release documentation
use_cases.description: Find unclear headings, weak instructions, and style-guide violations before publishing release notes or guides.

use_cases.title: Improve contributor drafts
use_cases.description: Give contributors concise feedback that matches Metabase tone, terminology, and formatting expectations.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Markdown file
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of one documentation file.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the docs-review skill to review this Markdown file for Metabase style, clarity, structure, and formatting issues. Return only numbered issues worth fixing.

prompt_templates.title: Review a documentation diff
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the docs-review skill to review this documentation diff. Focus on changed content, preserve sequential issue numbering, and include concise suggested fixes.

prompt_templates.title: Review a pull request
prompt_templates.scenario: GitHub PR review when MCP tools are available.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the docs-review skill in PR review mode. Start a pending review, inspect the diff, add every useful issue as a numbered comment, and submit one non-blocking review.

prompt_templates.title: Audit docs for publication readiness
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review across several documentation changes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the docs-review skill to evaluate these documentation changes for publication readiness. Prioritize reader impact, remove low-value comments, and number all remaining issues.

output_examples.input: A page says a setup step is easy and links the word here.
output_examples.output:
Issue 1: Condescending qualifier. The word easy can make readers feel blamed if setup fails. Remove it.

Issue 2: Vague link text. Replace here with descriptive link text that names the destination.

output_examples.input: A pull request changes headings and adds a command sequence.
output: Issue 1: Vague heading. Rewrite the heading so it states the action or result.
Issue 2: Command order is unclear. Put commands in execution order and explain context after the action.

output_examples.input: A draft uses formal corporate wording and refers to users throughout.
output_examples.output:
Issue 1: Formal tone. Replace corporate wording with direct language.

Issue 2: Audience term. Use people or companies where it fits the sentence.

best_practices: Provide the full diff or relevant Markdown file so the review has enough context.
Ask for only issues worth fixing when you need concise reviewer feedback.
Use PR review mode only when the required GitHub MCP tools are available.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill for non-Metabase style guides without adapting the guidance.
Do not ask it to rewrite entire documents when you need targeted review comments.
Do not expect it to verify product behavior that is not visible in the supplied changes.

faq.question: What does this skill review?
faq.answer: It reviews documentation changes for Metabase style, tone, structure, clarity, formatting, and reader impact.

faq.question: Can it post comments on GitHub pull requests?
faq.answer: Yes, when the GitHub MCP pending review tools are available in the active session.

faq.question: What happens without GitHub MCP tools?
faq.answer: The skill uses local review mode and returns numbered issues directly in the conversation.

faq.question: Does it rewrite documentation automatically?
faq.answer: No. It identifies issues and suggests concise fixes, but it does not apply edits by itself.

faq.question: Is it useful for non-Metabase documentation?
faq.answer: It can help with general clarity, but its rules are specific to Metabase documentation.

faq.question: Why are issues numbered?
faq.answer: Sequential numbering helps authors reference, discuss, and resolve specific review comments efficiently.
